steps.title: Identify the starting value
steps.content: At sea level ($x = 0$), the boiling point is 212°F. This is the $y$-intercept of the linear relationship.

steps.title: Find the slope from the given rate
steps.content: For every 550 feet above sea level, the boiling point drops by 1°F. The slope is therefore $-\frac{1}{550}$ degrees per foot.

steps.title: Form the equation using slope and y-intercept
steps.content:
For a linear relationship, $B = b + mx$, where $b = 212$ and $m = -\frac{1}{550}$:
$$B = 212 - \frac{x}{550}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
